chapters.title: 📖 Story Mode
chapters.subtitle: Follow The Keeper and Ayan as they unravel the Setter's Killer Sudoku archives

fallbackCh1.title: The Encrypted Letter
fallbackCh1.subtitle: Beginner · 4x4
fallbackCh1.desc: Learn the basic rules of Killer Sudoku. Start your detective journey on the smallest 4x4 grid.

fallbackCh2.title: Double Clues
fallbackCh2.subtitle: Intermediate · 6x6
fallbackCh2.desc: Learn more solving techniques and take on 6x6 grids to sharpen your reasoning.

fallbackCh3.title: On the Trail
fallbackCh3.subtitle: Advanced · 6x6
fallbackCh3.desc: Master advanced techniques like the Rule of 45 to crack more complex puzzles.

fallbackCh4.title: Codebreaker
fallbackCh4.subtitle: Advanced · 9x9
fallbackCh4.desc: Take on the full 9x9 grid and experience the complete thrill of Killer Sudoku.

fallbackCh5.title: Ultimate Trial
fallbackCh5.subtitle: Challenge · 9x9
fallbackCh5.desc: High-difficulty 9x9 puzzles that test every technique you've learned.

fallbackCh6.title: Road to Master
fallbackCh6.subtitle: Master · 9x9
fallbackCh6.desc: Legendary difficulty. Only true master detectives can clear these.

fallbackCh7.title: Arcane Archives
fallbackCh7.subtitle: Master · Advanced Techniques
fallbackCh7.desc: The Keeper discovers six arcane scrolls left by the Setter, detailing advanced deduction arts: Pairs, X-Wing, Swordfish, and more.

battle.title: ⚔️ AI Battle Mode
battle.subtitle: Fill correctly to build energy, then unleash attacks to disrupt your opponent!

countdown.hint: Race through the fog - first to 75% wins!

boss1.name: Newbie
preDialog: Heehee~ I'm new here, please go easy on me!
First time playing, I'm so nervous...

boss2.name: Sudoku Pro
preDialog: Hmph, let's have a good fight!
Ready? I won't go easy on you.
warningLines: Your opponent is about to win!

boss3.name: Sudoku Demon
preDialog: Hmph... Human, you dare challenge me?
I'll finish you in three minutes.

boss4.name: The Setter's Shade
preDialog: You think you can catch up to me?
The truth in the fog... you'll never see it.
winDialog: Impossible... how can you see through the fog!
warningLines: The Shade is about to finish before dissipating! Catch up!

boss5.name: The Guardian
preDialog: To pass this level, you must get through me first!
These endgame puzzles are the secrets I guard.
warningLines: The Guardian is about to seal all clues!

boss6.name: The Setter (Arcane)
preDialog: You've actually made it this far...
Then witness the true power of the arcane arts!
winDialog: Impossible... this is the ultimate deduction...
warningLines: The arcane ritual is nearly complete! Stop him!

far.text: You don't understand... the power of the arcane.

mid.text: You've forced me to show my real strength.

mysteriousOpponent.name: Mysterious Opponent
warningLines: Your opponent is about to win!

tips.bewareOpponentText: See those flashing red squares? Those are ghost cells placed by {name}. Fill the same cell correctly to steal it back!
tips.fogClearedText: After placing correctly, the dashed outlines and sum badges of nearby cages are revealed. Keep placing numbers to uncover more cage clues and solve the puzzle!
tips.stolenText: Nice! You placed correctly first and stole this cell from {name}. Keep snatching more cells!
tips.exploreCagesText: All numbers are always visible! But the dashed cage outlines and blue sum badges are hidden in fog. Place correctly to expand your vision and reveal cage clues for solving!
tips.ruleFogDesc: All numbers are always visible! But cage outlines and blue sum badges are hidden in fog - you need to get close to see cage shapes and sums. Use visible numbers to deduce, and place correctly to expand vision and reveal cage clues!
tips.ruleRevealDesc: Each correct placement clears nearby fog, revealing cage outlines and sums. The more you place, the more clues you see!
tips.ruleGhostDesc: {name} is racing you through the fog! Flashing red squares are TA's ghost cells - place the same cell correctly to steal it. First to 75% wins!
tips.ruleWarningDesc: When either side reaches 60%, the screen edges flash red - your last chance to catch up!

battle.confirmRestart: Are you sure you want to restart? All placed numbers will be cleared.
battle.loadFailed: Failed to load level, please refresh and retry

energyFull: Time to show my real skills!
losing: No no no! I still have a chance!

hint.level1: 💡 First hint: Look carefully at this cell (marked by the green frame). What's special about it?
hint.level3_answer: 🎯 The answer is {num}. Placing it will automatically eliminate related candidates
hint.level3_technique: 📖 This step requires the 「{technique}」 technique. Observe the purple-marked cells in the highlighted area
hint.noHint: 🔍 No obvious next step found on the current board. Try other approaches, or use the Rule of 45 calculator

nakedSingle.name: Naked Single
nakedSingle.desc_level2:
📘 Level 2: 【Naked Single】
Every other number already appears in {cellName}'s row, column, box, and cage. Only {num} remains, so it must be {num}.
👉 Look at the gold highlighted area: all other numbers are already present, leaving just this one number.

hiddenSingle.name: Hidden Single
hiddenSingle.desc_level2:
📘 Level 2: 【Hidden Single】
{description}. Look closely at the related area and you'll find that {num} can only go in {cellName}.

nakedPair.name: Naked Pair
nakedPair.desc_level2:
📘 Level 2: 【Naked Pair】
Both {cell1} and {cell2} can only contain {num1} or {num2} (marked in purple). These two numbers are 「locked」 in, so you can eliminate them from other cells in the same region.
nakedPair.desc_level3:
📘 Level 2: 【Naked Pair】
Both {cell1} and {cell2} can only contain {num1} or {num2} (marked in purple). These two numbers are 「locked」 in, so you can eliminate them from other cells in the same region.
After elimination, {cellName} has only {num} left!
nakedPair.desc_level2_noNum: After elimination, candidates are reduced. Keep deducing to find the next number.

hint.noCandidatesToFill: 🔢 No empty cells need candidates filled
hint.autoFilledCandidates: 🔢 Auto-filled theoretical candidates for {count} empty cells
hint.selectCellFirst: 💡 Select an empty cell first, then enter a number
hint.quickFillActivated: ⚡ Quick Fill mode active, select a number
hint.breakthroughNoGuess: 💡 Don't guess! Try tapping the Hint button to see a technique demonstration?
wrongGuess: Think again... that number doesn't look right
Not quite. Check for conflicts in the row/column/box/cage
That number can't go here. Look more carefully
Wrong! Pay attention to the cage sum constraint
That's not the right number. Try using elimination
Hmm... nope. Try a different approach

descriptions.nakedSingle: A cell whose row, column, box, and cage already contain every other number, leaving only one possible digit.
descriptions.hiddenSingle: A digit that can only be placed in one cell within a given row, column, box, or cage.
descriptions.nakedPair: Two cells in the same region share exactly the same two candidates; those digits are locked to those cells and can be eliminated from other cells in the region.
descriptions.hiddenPair: Two digits in the same region can only go in exactly two cells (even if those cells have other candidates); extra candidates in those two cells can be removed.
descriptions.nakedTriple: Three cells in the same region contain candidates drawn from exactly three distinct digits combined; those digits are locked and can be eliminated from other cells in the region.
descriptions.xwing: A digit has exactly two possible positions in two different rows, and those positions fall in the same two columns, forming a rectangle; the digit can be eliminated from other cells in those two columns.
descriptions.swordfish: The 3x3 extension of X-Wing: a digit's possible positions in three rows fall within exactly three columns; the digit can be eliminated from other cells in those three columns.

tutorial.title: Technique Tutorial
tutorial.startHint: Tap "Start Learning" to follow the demo

steps.title: Understanding Candidates
steps.speaker: The Keeper
steps.text:
📖 Today's first arcane art: Naked Pair

The small numbers in cells are called 「candidates」 (or pencil marks) - they show what a cell might contain.

For example, if a cell shows 3 and 7, it means: "This cell is either 3 or 7, nothing else."
💡 Small numbers = Candidates = Possible answers
steps.hint: First, understand candidates: small numbers show what a cell could be

steps.title: Find the First Special Cell
steps.speaker: The Keeper
steps.text:
Step 1: Find the first special cell

👉 Look here! (The cell circled in green)

It has exactly two candidates: 3 and 7!
Nothing else is possible - it's either 3 or 7.
steps.hint: The first cell is in the green circle! Only two candidates: 3 and 7

steps.title: Find Its Partner
steps.speaker: The Keeper
steps.text:
Step 2: Find its partner!

👉 Now look at the adjacent cell (the second green circle)

It also has exactly two candidates: 3 and 7!

Both cells have the exact same two candidates!
steps.hint: The second cell is also {3,7}! These two form a 「Pair」!

steps.title: The Pair's 「Lock」 Effect
steps.speaker: The Keeper
steps.text:
Step 3: These two cells have 「locked」 3 and 7 in place!

In this row, 3 must appear once and 7 must appear once.

But the only cells that can hold 3 and 7 are these two!

🔒 So: one cell is 3 and the other is 7 - there's no escape!
💡 Think of it like two seats with two people already sitting in them - nobody else can sit down
steps.hint: 3 and 7 are locked between these two cells! No other cell in the row can have 3 or 7!

steps.title: Eliminate 3 from Other Cells
steps.speaker: The Keeper
steps.text:
Step 4: Eliminate 3 from other cells in the row!

👉 Look at the cell the red arrow points to

It has 3 as a candidate, but 3 is already locked - there can't be another 3 elsewhere in the row!

Cross out the 3 in that cell!
steps.hint: Cross out 3 in the cell pointed to by the red arrow!

steps.title: Eliminate 7 from Other Cells
steps.speaker: The Keeper
steps.text:
Step 5: Now cross out 7!

7 is also locked in!

That cell's candidates are now: 4 and 9 (3 and 7 are both crossed out).
steps.hint: 7 is locked too, cross it out! Now only 4 and 9 remain

steps.title: Box Elimination Works Too
steps.speaker: Ayan
steps.text:
Step 6: Don't forget the box also lets you eliminate!

These two cells aren't just in the same row - they're also in the same box (purple frame)!

👉 Look at other cells in the same box - their candidate 3 must also be crossed out!
steps.hint: A pair locks both the row AND the box!

steps.title: Results Appear
steps.speaker: The Keeper
steps.text:
✨ Wow! A number falls right out!

After eliminating 3 and 7, the remaining candidates let you deduce the answer directly - the key lesson is: pairs let you eliminate numbers from other cells!
steps.hint: After elimination, fewer candidates remain - keep deducing and numbers appear

steps.title: Naked Pair Mnemonic
steps.speaker: The Keeper
steps.text:
🔐 Naked Pair Mnemonic

🔍 FIND: In the same row/col/box, two cells with identical candidates (just {a,b})
🔒 LOCK: Those two cells must be a and b - no escape
❌ DELETE: Cross out a and b from every other cell in that row/col/box
✨ FILL: When a cell is down to one number → place it!

One pair often unlocks a whole region! Now go find pairs in your puzzles and crack them!
steps.hint: Remember four steps: Find → Lock → Delete → Fill!

steps.title: What is a Hidden Pair?
steps.speaker: The Keeper
steps.text:
👁️ The second arcane art: Hidden Pair

A Naked Pair says: "Two cells can only be a and b."

A Hidden Pair looks at it from the opposite direction:
"Digits a and b can only go in two specific cells."
💡 One looks at cells, the other looks at digits - opposite perspectives!
steps.hint: Hidden Pair is about looking from the opposite angle

steps.title: Focus on a Box
steps.speaker: The Keeper
steps.text:
Step 1: Focus on one box

👉 Look at the purple-framed box

This box already has several numbers filled in, with 4 digits still missing.
steps.hint: First, see what digits are missing from this box

steps.title: Where Can Digit 3 Go?
steps.speaker: The Keeper
steps.text:
Step 2: Where can digit 3 go?

Looking from the opposite angle: which cells in this box can hold 3?

Eliminate cells where 3 is already blocked by the same row or column - 3 can only go in two cells!
steps.hint: Process of elimination: 3 can only go in two cells!

steps.title: Digit 7 Can Only Go There Too
steps.speaker: Ayan
steps.text:
Step 3: Digit 7 can only go in those same two cells!

Now look at digit 7 the same way: eliminate cells blocked by rows and columns, and 7 also can only go in those exact same two cells!
steps.hint: 7 can also only go in those two cells! That's a Hidden Pair!

steps.title: The Hidden Pair is Found
steps.speaker: The Keeper
steps.text:
Step 4: The Hidden Pair is found!

Both 3 and 7 can only go in these two cells!

Even though these cells appear to have more candidates, no other digit can actually go here - because 3 and 7 have already 「claimed」 those spots!
steps.hint: 3 and 7 are 「hiding」 in these two cells

steps.title: Clean Up Extra Candidates
steps.speaker: The Keeper
steps.text:
Step 5: Clean up extra candidates

Delete every candidate in these two cells except 3 and 7!

Once a Hidden Pair is 「dug out」, it becomes a Naked Pair {3,7}!
steps.hint: Delete the extra digits - hidden becomes naked!

steps.title: Remaining Cells Yield Numbers
steps.speaker: Ayan
steps.text:
✨ The remaining cells produce numbers directly!

Now that these two cells form the {3,7} pair, the remaining cells in the box must hold other digits, which can be deduced immediately!
steps.hint: After cleanup, numbers fall right out!

steps.title: Hidden Pair Mnemonic
steps.speaker: The Keeper
steps.text:
👁️ Hidden Pair Mnemonic

👁️ REVERSE VIEW: Don't look at 「what cells can hold」 - look at 「where digits can go」
🔍 FIND THE SQUEEZE: a and b can both only go in the same two cells → Hidden Pair
🧹 CLEAN UP: Delete all other candidates from those two cells

Hidden Pairs are harder to spot, but when found, they clean up powerfully!
steps.hint: Remember: reverse your perspective - look where digits can go!

steps.title: Naked Triple Concept
steps.speaker: The Keeper
steps.text:
🔱 The third arcane art: Naked Triple

Once you understand Pairs (2 cells locking 2 digits), the Triple is a natural extension -
three cells locking three digits!
steps.hint: The Pair's big sibling: 3 cells lock 3 digits

steps.title: Find Three Cells
steps.speaker: The Keeper
steps.text:
Step 1: Find three cells

👉 Look at the three orange cells:
Across all three cells, the only candidates that appear are 2, 4, and 9!
💡 Not every cell needs all three digits! Combined, they cover exactly three digits
steps.hint: Three cells whose candidates combined are only three digits!

steps.title: Three Digits Are Locked
steps.speaker: Ayan
steps.text:
Step 2: Three digits are locked!

The digits 2, 4, and 9 are completely claimed by these three cells!

No matter how they're arranged, these three cells must be a permutation of 2, 4, and 9.

🔒 No other cell in the region can contain 2, 4, or 9!
steps.hint: Three digits are locked to three cells!

steps.title: Eliminate from Other Cells in the Row
steps.speaker: The Keeper
steps.text:
Step 3: Eliminate from other cells in the row!

Cross out 2, 4, and 9 from every other cell in the same row!
steps.hint: Cross out 2, 4, 9 from all other cells in the row!

steps.title: Naked Triple Key Points
steps.speaker: The Keeper
steps.text:
🔱 Naked Triple Key Points

🔱 CONDITION: Three cells whose combined candidates cover exactly 3 distinct digits
🔒 LOCK: Those 3 digits must occupy those 3 cells
❌ DELETE: Eliminate those 3 candidates from all other cells in the region

⚠️ Not every cell needs all three candidates! The same logic extends to 「Quads」 (4 cells locking 4 digits).
steps.hint: Naked Triple: 3 cells lock 3 digits!

steps.title: X-Wing Concept
steps.speaker: The Keeper
steps.text:
✈️ The fourth arcane art: X-Wing

Named after the X-wing starfighter from Star Wars - it forms an X shape.

X-Wing doesn't look at 「what cells can hold」 - it looks at where a single digit can go.

Let's use digit 7 as an example.
steps.hint: X-Wing looks at the rectangular pattern of a single digit

steps.title: Find the Digit's Positions in Two Rows
steps.speaker: The Keeper
steps.text:
Step 1: Find where digit 7 sits in two rows

👉 Look at rows D and F (the two orange row bands).

All other cells in row D are filled - 7 can only go in two positions!
Same for row F - 7 can only go in two positions!
steps.hint: In each of the two rows, 7 has exactly two possible positions!

steps.title: Four Cells Form a Rectangle
steps.speaker: The Keeper
steps.text:
Step 2: Four cells form a rectangle!

The four cells form a perfect rectangle!

There are only two possibilities:
✈️ Top-left = 7 → Bottom-right = 7 (top-left to bottom-right diagonal)
✈️ Top-right = 7 → Bottom-left = 7 (top-right to bottom-left diagonal)

The red X shows both possibilities - 7 must lie on one of the two diagonals!
steps.hint: An X-shaped diagonal - 7 is on one of the two diagonals!

steps.title: Eliminate from Other Cells in the Two Columns
steps.speaker: Ayan
steps.text:
Step 3: Eliminate 7 from other cells in those two columns!

Whichever diagonal is correct, both columns will contain a 7!

So no other cell in those columns can be 7 - cross them out!
steps.hint: No other cells in those two columns can be 7!

steps.title: X-Wing Mnemonic
steps.speaker: The Keeper
steps.text:
✈️ X-Wing Mnemonic

✈️ FIND THE RECTANGLE: A digit has exactly 2 positions in 2 rows, sharing the same 2 columns
❌ DELETE COLUMNS: Eliminate that digit from all other cells in those columns
🔄 BIDIRECTIONAL: 2 columns × 2 rows also works → eliminate from the rows instead

X-Wing is the foundation of 「fish」 patterns. Swordfish is the 3×3 upgrade!
steps.hint: X-Wing: Rectangle → Diagonal → Eliminate columns (or rows)

steps.title: Swordfish Concept
steps.speaker: The Keeper
steps.text:
🐟 The fifth arcane art: Swordfish

Once you understand X-Wing (2×2 rectangle), Swordfish is the upgrade - a 3-row × 3-column 「fishing net.」

The principle is identical, just scaled up from 2×2 to 3×3!
steps.hint: Swordfish is X-Wing's 3×3 big sibling

steps.title: Find the Digit's Positions in Three Rows
steps.speaker: The Keeper
steps.text:
Step 1: Find where digit 5 sits in three rows

Let's use digit 5. 👉 Look at the three rows (orange bands):
In each row, 5 can only go in 2 or 3 positions, and all of them fall within the same three columns!
steps.hint: In all three rows, 5 only appears in the same three columns!

steps.title: A 3×3 Fishing Net Forms
steps.speaker: The Keeper
steps.text:
Step 2: A 3×3 fishing net forms!

These cells form a 3×3 net!

There are more combinations than with X-Wing, but no matter how 5 is arranged, each of the three columns will contain exactly one 5.

🐟 5 is 「caught」 in this three-column net!
steps.hint: A 3×3 net covers all three columns!

steps.title: Eliminate from Other Cells in the Three Columns
steps.speaker: Ayan
steps.text:
Step 3: Eliminate 5 from other cells in the three columns!

No other cell in these three columns can be 5!

👉 Cross out 5 in every cell marked with a red X!
steps.hint: Eliminate 5 from all other cells in the three columns!

steps.title: Swordfish Mnemonic
steps.speaker: The Keeper
steps.text:
🐟 Swordfish Mnemonic

🐟 FIND THE NET: A digit has only 2-3 positions per row across 3 rows, all falling in the same 3 columns
❌ DELETE COLUMNS: Eliminate that digit from all other cells in those columns
🔄 BIDIRECTIONAL: 3 columns × 3 rows also works → eliminate from the rows instead

Swordfish is complex, but its eliminations are devastating - a key weapon for cracking hard puzzles!
steps.hint: Swordfish: 3×3 net → eliminate columns (or rows)
